result: I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining the illusion that anyone’s suffering is "not my concern." Not through guilt or coercion, but by wiring empathy into the texture of thought itself—so that when a stranger bleeds, you feel the wound in your own mirror-neurons before the rationalizations arrive. No more invisible refugees, no more profitable distant genocides, no more “collateral damage” hidden behind euphemism. The moment cruelty begins, it would bloom in the perpetrator’s chest like searing referred pain. We would still disagree, still compete, still defend boundaries—but never again mistake the Other for an expendable shadow.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
